title: Pickled onions – make your own onions

seo.title: Pickled onions - make your own onions - Marinda Kook

html:
So I got a bunch of small onions at our local shop and decided to pickle them. The one thing about the onions is that you have to be patient. You have to do the work and then forget about them for at least 3 to 4 weeks. Then you may eat the deliciousness. Alongside a cheese board or in a salad, traditional pickled onions stay delicious.

 

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 1 kg small onions
  • 200 g coarse salt
  • enough water to cover the onions
  • 3 cloves
  • 8 peppercorns
  • 30 ml pickling spice
  • 20 ml chilli flakes – optional
  • 750 ml white grape vinegar
  • 200 ml brown sugar
  • thumb-sized piece of fresh ginger, cut into strips

NOW:

  1. peel the onions and sprinkle the coarse salt over and cover with water – leave it to stand like this overnight
  2. put all the rest of the ingredients in a saucepan and heat for 20 minutes
  3. rinse the onions off and place them in a sterilised jar
  4. pour the hot vinegar mixture over the onions right to the top
  5. seal and store for at least 3 weeks

I sterilise my jars by washing them on the hottest cycle in my dishwasher.

You can put fresh chillies, lemon peel or lemon leaves in the jar with the onions. It gives a nice colour and a lovely flavour.
